page: Amazon Web Services m6gd.4xlarge is a general-purpose instance powered by a 16-core AWS Graviton Neoverse-N1 ARMv8 processor running at 2.5 GHz. Built on the AWS Nitro hypervisor, it features 16 dedicated physical cores with a single thread per core, 64.0 GB of system memory, and a 950 GB local NVMe SSD instance store. The network configuration provides a baseline bandwidth of 5 Gbps.

Benchmark results place the m6gd.4xlarge in the average tier for multi-core operations and memory bandwidth. It achieves a stress-ng multi-core score of 24287.77 and a Geekbench multi-core score of 8538.0. Memory bandwidth is measured at 947.22 GB/sec for cached small-block reads and 151.25 GB/sec for large-block reads. However, single-core performance is relatively weak, as shown by a Geekbench single-core score of 941.0 and a single-threaded bzip3 decompression rate of 7.02 GB/sec.

The inclusion of local NVMe SSD storage alongside energy-efficient ARM64 cores provides a resource-dense profile suitable for workloads requiring high-speed local scratch space. It is well-suited for database operations, static web serving, and software compilation, as demonstrated by its 19.0 million operations per second in Redis SET benchmarks and average performance in Geekbench compiling tests. It is less optimal for single-threaded tasks or heavy LLM text generation.
description: Amazon Web Services m6gd.4xlarge is a general-purpose, ARM64-based instance powered by a 16-core AWS Graviton Neoverse-N1 processor running at 2.5 GHz. It features 64.0 GB of RAM, a 950 GB local NVMe SSD instance store, and 5 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. Operating on the AWS Nitro hypervisor, the instance delivers dedicated physical cores with one thread per core. Benchmarks indicate average multi-core and memory performance, with a stress-ng multi-core score of 24287.77 and a Redis SET throughput of 19.0 million operations per second, though single-core performance remains a relative bottleneck. The combination of local NVMe storage and balanced memory-to-CPU allocation makes this instance highly suitable for database operations, static web serving, and software compilation tasks.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m6gd.4xlarge is a general-purpose ARM64 instance featuring 16 dedicated Graviton cores, 64 GB RAM, and a 950 GB local NVMe SSD, delivering balanced multi-core performance for databases and web serving.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services m6gd.4xlarge is an ARM64 instance with 16 Graviton cores, 64 GB RAM, and a 950 GB NVMe SSD for general-purpose workloads.
tagline: An ARM64 general-purpose instance featuring dedicated physical cores and high-speed local NVMe SSD storage for balanced, data-intensive workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by a 16-core AWS Graviton Neoverse-N1 ARM64 processor
Equipped with 64.0 GB of RAM and a 950 GB local NVMe SSD
Delivers 5 Gbps baseline network bandwidth on the Nitro hypervisor
Strong multi-core performance with average database and web serving throughput
Best suited for database operations, software compilation, and static web serving
